Package org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor.manipulation

Class AbstractContextManipulation<E extends IInternalElement>

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.ui.eventbeditor.manipulation.AbstractAttributeManipulation

All Implemented Interfaces:
IAttributeManipulation
Direct Known Subclasses:
ExtendsContextAbstractContextNameAttributeManipulation, SeesContextNameAttributeManipulation

    • AbstractContextManipulation

  • Method Details

    • setDefaultValue

      public void setDefaultValue(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IAttributeManipulation
      Sets the value of the attribute for the given element to some default. This method is called at widget creation or when the user selects the UNDEFINED pseudo-entry in a combo-box.
      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if some problem occurred

    • getCurrentRootName

      private String getCurrentRootName(E element)

    • getPossibleValues

      public final String[] getPossibleValues(IRodinElement element, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
      Description copied from interface: IAttributeManipulation
      Gets the possible values of the attribute for the given element. This is used for filling the list of a combo-box. Returns null if the attribute is not intended to be edited by a combo-box widget.

      The returned list must include the current value of the attribute, otherwise the display of the combo box will not be accurate on Linux and Mac OS X.

      Parameters:
      element - an internal element
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting and cancellation are not desired
      Returns:
      an array of all values that the attribute can take, or null if unknown

    • getUsedContextNames

      public Set<String> getUsedContextNames(E element)
